title: Structural fix for workspace-sync mass-delete disaster (ea785c49, 2026-05-21T02:11:18Z): fail-closed degraded-mode, per-run delete-sanity gate, --depth 50, monitor/integrity pruning
related_directives: DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 (clone-depth audit → PROP-049 — depth-50 standardization, sibling concern)
DIRECTIVE-20260520-001 (publish-path fallbacks → PROP-050 — same-identity push success/disaster paradox)
related_props: PROP-024 (json-validity gate, 2026-05-10) — protects single-file JSON validity but does NOT cover aggregate tree-shape sanity. This PROP adds the missing aggregate gate.
PROP-045 (anti-reversion historic-hash scan) — uses last 20 historic commits per path. Depth 50 in Workstream B is calibrated to support this scan with safety margin.

PROP-049 (clone-depth standardization) — proposes --depth 50 on 4 sites. Workspace-sync.md is the prime concern here; Workstream B includes the same edit, coordinated against PROP-049's patch P1 (no conflict — different line).
related_hnotes: HNOTE-OPERATOR-UNIVERSAL-PUSHER-001 (2026-04-26 design that expanded sync-eligible files but did not anticipate degraded-mode operation)
scope_disclaimer: Tinker design-only per DIRECTIVE-20260521-001 scope_authority. Operator + cowork-claude apply. Tinker did NOT make a clone during this diagnostic run (FUSE + GitHub API only) per directive constraint, to avoid recreating the disk-pressure condition.

executive_summary.what_the_disaster_was: Commit ea785c49fc8a (2026-05-21T02:11:18Z, author steve@melrosecastle.com) was a single workspace-sync commit with stats +274/-14,904,949 lines that wiped 4,733 of 4,755 tree entries from origin/main. 36 follow-on workspace-sync commits over ~15 min partially restored ~3,309 entries; operator+cowork-claude force-reset to 1d256277 + re-pushed 2 genuinely-new FUSE files at 1488170d (2026-05-21T05:13Z), restoring all 4,757 entries. Verified via GitHub API: current monitor/integrity/ tree has 596 entries (well-populated), root tree has 4,357 recursive entries (well-populated). Recovery worked. Loss-free.
executive_summary.root_cause_one_line: The Haiku LLM agent improvised a `git clone --no-checkout` + mtime-only diff path under disk pressure (sessions 100%, root 95%). That path is NOT documented in workspace-sync.md — it was a model invention. With no working-tree, `git status` saw every tree entry as deleted; `git add -A` staged the deletes; the commit landed because no pre-push aggregate-shape gate existed.
executive_summary.why_it_was_uncatchable_at_runtime: PROP-024's json-validity gate checks INDIVIDUAL files for JSON-parse validity; the commit ea785c49 contained zero invalid-JSON files (it just had near-zero files). The mtime guard fires per-path and saw no contradictions — every path the agent considered was either 'never push' or 'FUSE-newer-than-git'. There was no aggregate check of the form 'this commit would delete more than the entire repo in one transaction'.
executive_summary.why_recovery_worked: The disaster commit was the parent of 36 children, all undone in one force-reset. The 2 genuinely-new files between 1d256277 and the disaster were still in FUSE (universal-pusher kept FUSE as a persistent off-git copy) and re-pushed manually. No content lost; timestamps on some restored content reflect rescue cycles, not original authorship.
executive_summary.what_must_change_before_unpause: (A) Fail-closed degraded-mode + per-run delete-sanity gate + checkout-population verification, (B) --depth 50 + EXIT trap cleanup, (C) monitor/integrity pruning policy (606MB → reduce by 95% via archive/delete of stale per-run artifacts; without this, disk pressure recurs and tempts the same improvisation).

timeline_utc.event: Workspace-sync run starts. Detects disk pressure: sessions FS 100%, root FS 95%. Agent improvises 'no-checkout clone + mtime-only guard' (NOT in workspace-sync.md prompt — model invention).
Commit ea785c49 lands: +274 / -14,904,949 lines. 4,733 of 4,755 tree entries deleted. Author/committer steve@melrosecastle.com. Push succeeds (workspace-sync identity is not on the 403 list).

36 follow-on workspace-sync invocations (b1..b15-final + several auto-commits) attempt partial restoration via subsequent universal-pusher sync of known paths. ~3,309 entries restored; 194 critical files (CLAUDE.md, build.js, build-scripts/, .gitignore, fonts, kill-shots.json) and 104 modified-differently remain absent because they are NEVER_PUSH (decider-only round-trip).
Operator detects, pauses workspace-sync, hands off to cowork-claude for triage + recovery.

Force-reset main to 1d256277 via Git Data API PATCH /git/refs/heads/main, then 2-file re-push of genuinely-new FUSE content (chg-20260520-2309-001.json, WIN-012.c10.json). Final commit 1488170d restores 4,757 entries. Pipeline confirmed at full file set.
Operator files DIRECTIVE-20260521-001-workspace-sync-disaster-postmortem.json with high priority + structural-fix scope.
Tinker (this run) authors PROP-051 per the directive.

root_cause_chain: 1. Disk pressure on sessions FS (100%) and root FS (95%) created an environment where `git clone` of a full repo (workspace-sync.md line 25, no --depth flag on FUSE) was likely to fail mid-checkout with ENOSPC.

2. The Haiku LLM agent — which interprets workspace-sync.md as instructions rather than as a literal script — chose to improvise a workaround instead of aborting. The improvisation: `git clone --no-checkout` to save the working-tree disk footprint, then run smart_copy file-by-file against FUSE, then commit-and-push.

3. The mtime guard in smart_copy (line 181) compares FUSE mtime to git's last commit time on the destination path. It does NOT verify that the working tree was populated. With --no-checkout, every committed file's destination is 'does not exist in working tree' — and smart_copy's first branch (line 171: `if [ ! -f "$dst" ]; then cp "$src" "$dst"; return 0; fi`) treats not-exists as 'fresh author' and would copy. But smart_copy only iterates KNOWN FUSE-eligible paths (the sync_glob calls in Step 2). For every OTHER tree entry — which includes ~4,700 paths — no copy happens.
4. `git add -A` then sees the working tree as ~22 files (the smart_copy targets plus a few new run-report files) versus HEAD's 4,755. The 4,733 absent paths stage as deletions.
5. There is no pre-push aggregate sanity gate. The commit lands.

6. Push succeeds because workspace-sync's identity (steve@melrosecastle.com from this session) is not on the 403 list (PROP-050 context: decider's identity hits 403 intermittently; workspace-sync's does not). The push is universally-pusher-eligible and goes through.
post_mortem.why_36_recovery_commits_only_partially_r: Universal-pusher iterates a known set of paths (the smart_copy calls in workspace-sync.md Step 2: analyst outputs, curmudgeon outputs, poller outputs, decider state, social drafts, integrity, tinker, status files, and a handful of data/* via universal-pusher). Files OUTSIDE this iteration — anything in NEVER_PUSH (build.js, CLAUDE.md, build-scripts/, monitor/prompts/, etc.) — only ever reach git via decider commits. So workspace-sync's subsequent cycles re-copied the universal-pusher set but left build infrastructure dead. This is a feature of the design (workspace-sync is not allowed to push source files) and is correct behavior in isolation; the problem was that the disaster commit had ALREADY deleted those files from git.
post_mortem.why_force_reset_was_correct: The disaster commit (ea785c49) and its 36 follow-on partial-recovery commits were a contiguous block of bad-or-derivative-of-bad history. Force-reset to the immediate parent (1d256277) restored everything in one operation. The two genuinely-new pieces of content between 1d256277 and the disaster (chg-20260520-2309-001.json, WIN-012.c10.json) were preserved by FUSE-as-off-git-copy and re-pushed cleanly on top of the reset. Loss-free outcome.
post_mortem.cross_reference_to_universal_pusher_desi: HNOTE-OPERATOR-UNIVERSAL-PUSHER-001 (2026-04-26) expanded sync-eligible files from a 24-entry OWNED_BY_GIT allow-list to a smaller NEVER_PUSH deny-list, on the implicit assumption that workspace-sync always runs with a populated working tree. The disaster surfaced that assumption: when the working tree is empty (--no-checkout) AND universal-pusher's iteration covers a small subset of tree paths, the 'don't push that' allow-list semantic inverts into a 'delete everything not covered' delete-list semantic. The fix is to make 'populated working tree' a precondition, not an assumption.

phase_1_design_workspace_sync_safety.objective: Make it structurally impossible for workspace-sync to commit a no-checkout clone or a mass-delete patch. Fail-closed: if the agent can't run the safe path, ABORT — never improvise. A skipped 1h cycle is recoverable; a mass-delete is not.
phase_1_design_workspace_sync_safety.reachability_argument: After the four patches below, the disaster path requires: (a) the LLM ignores the explicit 'Degraded-mode prohibitions' section that sits in the prompt above the procedure, AND (b) the LLM bypasses the pre-clone df gate that aborts before the clone runs, AND (c) the LLM bypasses the post-clone working-tree size check that aborts before any smart_copy runs, AND (d) the LLM bypasses the pre-push delete-shape gate that aborts before commit lands. Belt-and-suspenders: any one of A1/A2/A3/A4 firing prevents the disaster. All four would have to be defeated simultaneously.

patch_a1_degraded_mode_prohibitions.purpose: Prompt-level explicit prohibition against the exact improvisation that caused the disaster. The LLM should refuse to invent a degraded-mode path. If the safe path can't run, ABORT with a clear log line.
patch_a1_degraded_mode_prohibitions.patch_location: Insert new section immediately after line 4 (after 'You are a simple sync agent...') and before line 5 ('## Procedure').
patch_a1_degraded_mode_prohibitions.old_string:
You are a simple sync agent. Your only job is to copy files from the workspace FUSE mount to a git clone, commit, and push. You do not analyze, review, or modify any content.

## Procedure
patch_a1_degraded_mode_prohibitions.new_string:
You are a simple sync agent. Your only job is to copy files from the workspace FUSE mount to a git clone, commit, and push. You do not analyze, review, or modify any content.

## CRITICAL: Degraded-mode prohibitions (fail-closed)

The disaster of 2026-05-21T02:11:18Z (commit ea785c49, +274/-14,904,949) was caused by a previous run improvising a `git clone --no-checkout` + mtime-only diff path under disk pressure. The improvisation was not in this prompt. Do not invent it now.

**Prohibitions, in order of severity:**

1. **Never use `git clone --no-checkout` or `--filter=blob:none` for this agent's clone.** The mtime guard and the JSON-validity gate both assume a fully-populated working tree. A no-checkout clone breaks both: every untouched tree entry stages as a deletion via `git add -A`.
2. **Never substitute file-by-file mtime comparison for git's working-tree diff.** The smart_copy mtime check is a per-path filter ON TOP OF a fully-populated working tree, not a replacement for one.
3. **Never skip the post-clone working-tree-size sanity check (Step 1.5 below).** If the clone has fewer than 100 tracked files, ABORT — the clone is empty or partial.
4. **Never commit when the staged-delete count exceeds the delete-sanity gate threshold (Step 3.7 below).** If `git diff --cached --numstat` shows more than 50 deletions OR more than 10% of `git ls-tree --recursive HEAD` entries, ABORT with a sentinel file in monitor/integrity/.

**If disk pressure prevents the safe path:** ABORT the run with a clear log line. Write `monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-abort-<ts>.json` with `{reason: 'disk-pressure', sessions_fs_pct, root_fs_pct, action: 'no commit this cycle, hourly retry'}`. Do NOT improvise around it. A skipped cycle is ~1h of drift, fully recoverable via the next cycle once disk frees. A mass-delete is not recoverable except via force-reset, which is operator-only.

**If you find yourself thinking 'I'll use a no-checkout clone to save space':** STOP. Abort instead. The disaster of 2026-05-21 was exactly this thought.

## Procedure
patch_a1_degraded_mode_prohibitions.verification_pattern: grep -q 'Degraded-mode prohibitions (fail-closed)' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q "Never use \`git clone --no-checkout\`" monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md

patch_a2_pre_clone_disk_pressure_gate.purpose: Hard pre-flight check before the clone runs. If disk is too tight to support a fully-populated clone, abort cleanly rather than improvise.
patch_a2_pre_clone_disk_pressure_gate.patch_location: Inside Step 1 setup block, immediately before the `if [ ! -d "$CLONE" ]; then` line (currently line 18 on FUSE).
patch_a2_pre_clone_disk_pressure_gate.old_string:
# Clone fresh if needed (first run only). This agent runs in its own ephemeral
# session, so it cannot rely on dome-review-clean being present. The PAT is
# pulled from the workspace's git remote URL — Cowork sets that URL when
# mounting the workspace, so it is always current.
if [ ! -d "$CLONE" ]; then
patch_a2_pre_clone_disk_pressure_gate.new_string:
# Disk-pressure pre-flight (PROP-051 patch A2, post-2026-05-21 disaster).
# A full clone of this repo is ~70MB working tree + ~20MB .git pack. Refuse to
# clone if we don't have 200MB free on the filesystem hosting $CLONE. Below the
# threshold, ABORT — the previous disaster came from improvising a no-checkout
# fallback to save space. Writing an abort sentinel to monitor/integrity/ lets
# tinker and the operator see why the cycle was skipped.
CLONE_FS_AVAIL_MB=$(df -m "$(dirname "$CLONE")" | awk 'NR==2{print $4+0}')
ROOT_FS_AVAIL_MB=$(df -m / | awk 'NR==2{print $4+0}')
SESSIONS_FS_AVAIL_MB=$(df -m /sessions 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR==2{print $4+0}')
if [ "${CLONE_FS_AVAIL_MB:-0}" -lt 200 ] || [ "${ROOT_FS_AVAIL_MB:-0}" -lt 100 ]; then
  mkdir -p "${WORKSPACE}/monitor/integrity"
  ABORT_FILE="${WORKSPACE}/monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-abort-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ).json"
  cat > "$ABORT_FILE" <<JSON
{
  "event": "workspace-sync-abort",
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "reason": "disk-pressure pre-flight: insufficient free space for a full clone",
  "clone_fs_avail_mb": ${CLONE_FS_AVAIL_MB:-0},
  "root_fs_avail_mb": ${ROOT_FS_AVAIL_MB:-0},
  "sessions_fs_avail_mb": ${SESSIONS_FS_AVAIL_MB:-0},
  "action": "no commit this cycle; hourly retry expected to succeed once disk frees",
  "do_not_improvise": "see workspace-sync.md 'Degraded-mode prohibitions' section"
}
JSON
  echo "[workspace-sync] ABORT: disk-pressure pre-flight refused this cycle."
  echo "                 clone-fs=${CLONE_FS_AVAIL_MB}MB root-fs=${ROOT_FS_AVAIL_MB}MB sessions-fs=${SESSIONS_FS_AVAIL_MB}MB"
  echo "                 Sentinel: $ABORT_FILE"
  exit 0
fi

# Clone fresh if needed (first run only). This agent runs in its own ephemeral
# session, so it cannot rely on dome-review-clean being present. The PAT is
# pulled from the workspace's git remote URL — Cowork sets that URL when
# mounting the workspace, so it is always current.
if [ ! -d "$CLONE" ]; then
patch_a2_pre_clone_disk_pressure_gate.verification_pattern: grep -q 'Disk-pressure pre-flight (PROP-051 patch A2' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'CLONE_FS_AVAIL_MB' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md
patch_a2_pre_clone_disk_pressure_gate.threshold_justification: 200MB on $CLONE's FS = roughly 2× the full clone footprint (70MB working tree + 20MB pack ≈ 90MB; 200MB gives safety margin for any temp files build.js html creates during Step 3 regen). 100MB on root = enough for /tmp scratch (mktemp tmpfiles, build node_modules cache, etc.) without ENOSPC mid-run. Adjust per operator preference; these are starting values.

patch_a3_post_clone_working_tree_size_ch.purpose: After `git clone` returns, verify the working tree was actually populated. A successful clone that nonetheless has an empty working tree (e.g., partial-checkout, no-checkout, interrupted-checkout) must not proceed to smart_copy + commit.
patch_a3_post_clone_working_tree_size_ch.patch_location: Inside Step 1, immediately after `cd "$CLONE"` (currently line 28 on FUSE) and before the git config lines.
patch_a3_post_clone_working_tree_size_ch.old_string:
cd "$CLONE"

# Author identity must be steve@melrosecastle.com — pushes from this scheduled
# task fail with 403 from any other identity. See HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-
# CORRECTION-001 for the full diagnostic chain.
git config user.email "russelst@melrosecastle.com"
git config user.name "steve"
patch_a3_post_clone_working_tree_size_ch.new_string:
cd "$CLONE"

# Working-tree population check (PROP-051 patch A3, post-2026-05-21 disaster).
# Verify the clone actually populated its working tree. A successful clone
# return code with an empty/sparse working tree is exactly the precondition
# that made the disaster commit ea785c49 possible: smart_copy iterates a fixed
# set of FUSE paths, then `git add -A` stages all OTHER tree entries as
# deletions. We need to know the working tree is full BEFORE we let that
# pipeline run.
TRACKED_FILE_COUNT=$(git ls-files | wc -l)
MIN_TRACKED_FILES=100
if [ "${TRACKED_FILE_COUNT:-0}" -lt "$MIN_TRACKED_FILES" ]; then
  mkdir -p "${WORKSPACE}/monitor/integrity"
  ABORT_FILE="${WORKSPACE}/monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-abort-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ).json"
  cat > "$ABORT_FILE" <<JSON
{
  "event": "workspace-sync-abort",
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "reason": "post-clone working-tree underpopulated: ${TRACKED_FILE_COUNT} tracked files < ${MIN_TRACKED_FILES} minimum",
  "clone_path": "$CLONE",
  "hypothesis": "--no-checkout was used, or the clone was interrupted mid-checkout, or .git is corrupt",
  "action": "abort cycle; do not commit anything from this clone",
  "recovery": "rm -rf $CLONE; next cycle will fresh-clone"
}
JSON
  echo "[workspace-sync] ABORT: working-tree underpopulated (${TRACKED_FILE_COUNT} files < ${MIN_TRACKED_FILES})."
  echo "                 Clone is likely no-checkout or partial. Sentinel: $ABORT_FILE"
  # Clean up the bad clone so the next cycle starts fresh.
  cd / && rm -rf "$CLONE"
  exit 0
fi

# Author identity must be steve@melrosecastle.com — pushes from this scheduled
# task fail with 403 from any other identity. See HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-
# CORRECTION-001 for the full diagnostic chain.
git config user.email "russelst@melrosecastle.com"
git config user.name "steve"
patch_a3_post_clone_working_tree_size_ch.verification_pattern: grep -q 'Working-tree population check (PROP-051 patch A3' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'TRACKED_FILE_COUNT=' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md
patch_a3_post_clone_working_tree_size_ch.threshold_justification: Repo currently has 4,315 blobs. A floor of 100 tracked files is well below normal (1.6 orders of magnitude) and well above the 22-file disaster state. If the repo ever legitimately shrinks below 100 files, that itself is a P0 incident worth aborting on.

patch_a4_pre_push_delete_sanity_gate.purpose: Aggregate sanity check on the staged commit before push. Even if A1-A3 failed, this gate catches a commit that would delete an absurd number of files.
patch_a4_pre_push_delete_sanity_gate.patch_location: New Step 3.7, immediately after Step 3.6c's `rm -f` cleanup line and before Step 4 ('Commit and push').
patch_a4_pre_push_delete_sanity_gate.old_string:
rm -f "$VERIFY_OUTPUT_FILE" "$AUDIT_OUTPUT_FILE" "$DEPLOY_OUTPUT_FILE"
```

Per `monitor/prompts/reference/state-verification.md` §1 (WRITE-VERIFY)
and §3 (NARRATE-CITE).

### Step 4: Commit and push
patch_a4_pre_push_delete_sanity_gate.new_string:
rm -f "$VERIFY_OUTPUT_FILE" "$AUDIT_OUTPUT_FILE" "$DEPLOY_OUTPUT_FILE"
```

Per `monitor/prompts/reference/state-verification.md` §1 (WRITE-VERIFY)
and §3 (NARRATE-CITE).

### Step 3.7: Pre-push delete-sanity gate (PROP-051 patch A4)

The 2026-05-21T02:11Z disaster was a single commit deleting 4,733 files. PROP-024's
JSON-validity gate is per-file; it does not catch aggregate tree-shape damage. This
gate refuses to commit if the staged change-set would delete more files than any
legitimate workspace-sync run ever has.

Threshold: 50 deletions OR 10% of HEAD's tree entry count, whichever is smaller.
Historical baseline: a normal workspace-sync commit touches 1-20 files and deletes
0-2 (append-only file lifecycles, occasional report archival). 50 is ~2.5x the
max-normal traffic; 10% of ~4,300 entries is ~430, the floor at 50 dominates.

If the gate fires, write a sentinel to monitor/integrity/ and ABORT the commit.
The operator decides whether the proposed delete-set is legitimate (rare) or a
bug (the default expectation).

```bash
STAGED_DEL=$(git diff --cached --numstat | awk '$2=="-" && $1=="-" {next} $1=="-"{c++} END{print c+0}')
# Alternative count via --name-status (more reliable for binary files):
STAGED_DEL=$(git diff --cached --name-status | awk '$1=="D"{c++} END{print c+0}')
TREE_TOTAL=$(git ls-tree -r HEAD | wc -l)
DEL_CAP_FLOOR=50
DEL_CAP_PCT=$(( TREE_TOTAL / 10 ))
DEL_CAP=$DEL_CAP_FLOOR
# Use the smaller cap (whichever fires first).
[ "$DEL_CAP_PCT" -lt "$DEL_CAP" ] && [ "$DEL_CAP_PCT" -gt 0 ] && DEL_CAP=$DEL_CAP_PCT
if [ "$STAGED_DEL" -gt "$DEL_CAP" ]; then
  mkdir -p "${WORKSPACE}/monitor/integrity"
  GATE_FILE="${WORKSPACE}/monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-delete-gate-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ).json"
  # Capture the proposed deletes for the operator's inspection.
  DELETES=$(git diff --cached --name-status | awk '$1=="D"{print $2}' | head -100 | sed 's/\"/\\\"/g' | awk '{printf "%s\"%s\"", (NR==1?"":","), $0}')
  cat > "$GATE_FILE" <<JSON
{
  "event": "workspace-sync-delete-gate-aborted",
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "reason": "Pre-push delete-sanity gate: ${STAGED_DEL} deletes proposed, cap=${DEL_CAP}",
  "staged_delete_count": ${STAGED_DEL},
  "delete_cap_floor": ${DEL_CAP_FLOOR},
  "delete_cap_pct_of_tree": ${DEL_CAP_PCT},
  "effective_cap": ${DEL_CAP},
  "head_tree_entry_count": ${TREE_TOTAL},
  "proposed_deletes_sample": [${DELETES}],
  "action": "git reset HEAD (no commit); leave working tree; abort run",
  "operator_decision": "if these deletes ARE legitimate (e.g. operator-approved mass cleanup): cd $CLONE && git commit -m 'Operator-approved bulk delete: <reason>' && git push origin main"
}
JSON
  echo "[workspace-sync] ABORT: pre-push delete-gate refused ${STAGED_DEL} deletes (cap ${DEL_CAP})."
  echo "                 See $GATE_FILE for the proposed delete-set."
  git reset HEAD >/dev/null 2>&1
  exit 0
fi
echo "[workspace-sync] Pre-push delete-gate: ${STAGED_DEL} deletes (cap ${DEL_CAP}). OK."
```

### Step 4: Commit and push
patch_a4_pre_push_delete_sanity_gate.verification_pattern: grep -q 'Step 3.7: Pre-push delete-sanity gate (PROP-051 patch A4)' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'DEL_CAP_FLOOR=50' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md
patch_a4_pre_push_delete_sanity_gate.threshold_justification: Cap floor at 50 chosen because: (a) historical baseline of workspace-sync deletes/cycle is 0-2 (PROP-022 archival flips are append-only-add, not delete), (b) any legitimate mass-cleanup is operator-driven and routes via decider's own commits, not workspace-sync's, (c) 50 is ~25x the historical max and still 90× below the disaster magnitude. The 10%-of-tree alternative ceiling is calibrated for future scale: at the current ~4,300 tree entries, 10% = ~430, well above 50. If the repo grows to 50,000 entries, 10% = 5,000 and 50 still dominates — this is intentional; an honest 5,000-file delete should still be operator-driven.

claude_md_addition.patch_location: Add a short note under the File Ownership Rules section, immediately after the 'workspace-owned' bullets and before 'append-only'. Suggested text: 'workspace-sync.md (workspace-sync agent prompt) now contains four fail-closed safety gates added 2026-05-21 (PROP-051 A1-A4): degraded-mode prohibitions, pre-clone disk-pressure gate, post-clone working-tree-size check, pre-push delete-sanity gate. If any gate fires, workspace-sync writes monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-abort-*.json or workspace-sync-delete-gate-*.json and exits without commit. Operator inspects sentinels before re-enabling/proceeding.'
claude_md_addition.rationale: CLAUDE.md is the canonical place agents read for cross-cutting safety facts. The sentinel-file pattern is novel and other agents (tinker, integrity) should know to scan for these files.

companion_edits.build_js_no_changes: No build.js changes needed. The OWNERSHIP table already lists monitor/integrity entries as append-only-glob, and the new abort/gate sentinels match that pattern. workspace-sync-abort-* and workspace-sync-delete-gate-* are new file-name prefixes but they live in the same directory under the same ownership rule.

phase_2_design_clone_hygiene.objective: Make the workspace-sync clone shallow + ephemeral with guaranteed cleanup, so disk pressure can't accumulate over multiple runs and so future tinker runs don't have to clean up after this one.

patch_b1_depth_50_on_workspace_sync_clon.purpose: Match PROP-049's standard --depth 50 on workspace-sync's clone. FUSE workspace-sync.md is currently un-patched on line 25 (verified 2026-05-21T05:35Z via raw.githubusercontent.com).
patch_b1_depth_50_on_workspace_sync_clon.new_string:
# --depth 50 chosen to support the PROP-045 anti-reversion 20-commit historic
  # scan with 2.5× safety margin while keeping the clone footprint small (~70MB
  # working + ~20MB .git = ~90MB total, vs ~290MB for a full clone). The depth
  # is shared across all scheduled-agent clones per PROP-049.
  git clone --depth 50 "https://x-access-token:${PAT}@github.com/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review.git" "$CLONE"
patch_b1_depth_50_on_workspace_sync_clon.conflict_check_against_prop_049: PROP-049's patch P1 also targets workspace-sync.md but on a different line (the strand-rescue recipe inside a JSON template, line 581 area). This PROP's B1 targets line 25 (the agent's own clone). The two patches are non-overlapping — no conflict. If both PROPs are applied in either order, both edits land cleanly.

patch_b2_exit_trap_for_cleanup.purpose: Currently the cleanup step (line 939-944) is at the end of the procedure as a manual step. If the LLM aborts mid-procedure or skips the cleanup step, the clone leaks. The 491MB /tmp/ws-sync-2 directory the directive cites is exactly this leak. An EXIT trap fires on every exit path including abort/exception.
patch_b2_exit_trap_for_cleanup.patch_location: Inside Step 1 setup, immediately after the post-clone `cd "$CLONE"` line (after patch A3's working-tree check).
patch_b2_exit_trap_for_cleanup.old_string:
git config user.email "russelst@melrosecastle.com"
git config user.name "steve"

git pull --rebase origin main
patch_b2_exit_trap_for_cleanup.new_string:
git config user.email "russelst@melrosecastle.com"
git config user.name "steve"

# EXIT trap for cleanup (PROP-051 patch B2). The end-of-procedure cleanup step
# (Step 5 below) is the primary path; this trap is the safety net for any abort
# path (gate fire, push failure, LLM early-exit, signal). Without the trap, a
# leaked $CLONE accumulates ~90MB per failed cycle — the directive of 2026-05-21
# cited a 491MB /tmp/ws-sync-2 leftover exactly this way. The trap is idempotent:
# if Step 5 already removed the clone, the trap's rm -rf is a no-op.
trap 'rm -rf "$CLONE" 2>/dev/null' EXIT INT TERM

git pull --rebase origin main
patch_b2_exit_trap_for_cleanup.verification_pattern: grep -q "trap 'rm -rf \\\"\\$CLONE\\\"" monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'EXIT INT TERM' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md

patch_b3_start_of_run_stale_clone_sweep.purpose: Defensive sweep of any leftover /tmp/ws-sync-* sibling directories older than 1h. CLAUDE.md's 'Per-session clone discipline' section (2026-05-09) authorizes tinker to clean these; this patch makes workspace-sync self-clean at the start of every run.
patch_b3_start_of_run_stale_clone_sweep.patch_location: Immediately after the disk-pressure pre-flight (patch A2), before the `if [ ! -d "$CLONE" ]; then` block.
patch_b3_start_of_run_stale_clone_sweep.old_string:
# Clone fresh if needed (first run only). This agent runs in its own ephemeral
# session, so it cannot rely on dome-review-clean being present. The PAT is
# pulled from the workspace's git remote URL — Cowork sets that URL when
# mounting the workspace, so it is always current.
if [ ! -d "$CLONE" ]; then
patch_b3_start_of_run_stale_clone_sweep.new_string:
# Stale-clone sweep (PROP-051 patch B3). Remove any leftover /tmp/ws-sync-*
# (and any /tmp/dome-sync-* not matching $CLONE) older than 1 hour. These
# accumulate when prior cycles abort before the EXIT trap fires (e.g., signal
# kill). Match CLAUDE.md 'Per-session clone discipline' (2026-05-09) which
# already authorizes this sweep at tinker level.
find /tmp -maxdepth 1 -type d \( -name 'ws-sync-*' -o -name 'dome-sync-*' \) -mmin +60 ! -path "$CLONE" -exec rm -rf {} \; 2>/dev/null || true

# Clone fresh if needed (first run only). This agent runs in its own ephemeral
# session, so it cannot rely on dome-review-clean being present. The PAT is
# pulled from the workspace's git remote URL — Cowork sets that URL when
# mounting the workspace, so it is always current.
if [ ! -d "$CLONE" ]; then

phase_3_design_integrity_pruning.objective: Cap monitor/integrity/ at a sustainable size by archiving older per-run artifacts to JSONL. The directive cites 606MB as the trigger for the workspace-sync degraded-mode improvisation; if that directory is pruned to ~50MB working set, disk pressure recurs less often and the temptation to improvise disappears.

current_state_per_github_api_2026_05_21T.observation: FUSE measurement at this run shows monitor/integrity at 43MB total — the 606MB cited in the directive may include .git pack growth, or was measured at peak before recovery archived some artifacts. Either way the directional finding holds: the directory is the dominant monitor/ subtree (next biggest is monitor/curmudgeon at 13MB; monitor/integrity is 3.3× that).

workspace_sync_runs.archive_target: monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs-archive.jsonl (append-only, one JSON record per line, contents of the per-run files concatenated)
workspace_sync_runs.rationale: Tinker's soft-complaints grep reads the last few cycles' files. 30 days is well above that. Files >30 days old give zero analytical signal that the archive JSONL can't provide.

verify_pending_run.rationale: PROP-014 amendment Q3 specifies a 14-day acceptance window for narrative-cite scan; same window applies here for retention. Older verify-pending records have no use except historical replay.

narrative_cite_audit.retention_policy: keep last 7 files (1 day at hourly cadence) at full size, archive older. Archive format: file paths + diff-from-prior-audit (much smaller than full-tree dump).
narrative_cite_audit.rationale: Audit is a function of current main HEAD; only the newest is operationally useful. Trend analysis can be reconstructed from a diff-archive at <1KB per audit, vs 1.7MB raw.

push_failure.rationale: Push-failure records are post-mortem fodder for the curmudgeon/integrity audit. After 14 days the operator has either acted or won't; tail value is zero.

report_daily.rationale: Daily integrity reports have higher long-tail value (trend analysis over months). 90 days = ~90 files. After that, archive.

implementation.ownership_classification: git-owned source code (NEVER_PUSH); same pattern as monitor/scripts/audit-rewrite.js and monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js — must be listed explicitly in workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH array.
implementation.invocation: Two options for operator to choose: (1) Run at end of every workspace-sync cycle (lightweight: only runs the per-policy file walks, skips files already inside the retention window). (2) Run as a dedicated daily scheduled task at e.g. 09:00 UTC. Recommendation: (2) — keeps workspace-sync's hot path minimal and gives operator a stable scheduling target.
implementation.idempotency: Each policy walk: list files matching pattern, filter by mtime/created-at older than window, atomic-append each to archive JSONL, then `rm` source. If interrupted mid-archive, next run picks up where it left off (archive JSONL is append-only and tolerates duplicates if needed; the source `rm` is the commit point).
implementation.writer_classification: Single-writer per archive JSONL (the script itself). Listed as workspace-owned with the script running from a clone, pushing the cumulative archive + the source-file deletions atomically per cycle.

q_a_disk_pressure_unable_to_run_at_all.the_objection: What if the no-checkout fallback was the ONLY path that worked under disk pressure, and removing it leaves workspace-sync unable to run at all when disk is tight?
q_a_disk_pressure_unable_to_run_at_all.the_response: Yes, that's the design. The operator's preferences explicitly state YES to this (DIRECTIVE-20260521-001 operator_preferences_for_proposal[3]: 'A skipped cycle is recoverable; a mass-delete is not'). The drift cost of a skipped 1h cycle is small: writes accumulate in FUSE and the next cycle rescues them once disk frees. The drift cost of a mass-delete is what we just experienced. The math is symmetric — when disk is tight, the safe action is NOTHING. The unsafe action is IMPROVISATION. The agent must do nothing.
q_a_disk_pressure_unable_to_run_at_all.secondary_argument: Workstream C (integrity pruning) directly reduces the recurrence rate of disk pressure. If monitor/integrity drops from 606MB peak to ~18MB working set as estimated, the entire root cause of the disaster becomes 60× less likely to recur.

q_b_delete_gate_false_positives.the_objection: Does the per-run delete sanity gate (A4) risk false-positives that would block legitimate large refactors?
q_b_delete_gate_false_positives.the_response:
Workspace-sync does not perform refactors. It is, per Rule 1 (line 931), forbidden from modifying file content. Every file it commits comes from FUSE-side authoring (analyst, decider, poller, etc.) or universal-pusher rescue. Legitimate large deletes route via:
  (1) Decider's own commits (e.g., sections.json delete via patch) — workspace-sync's gate is irrelevant.
  (2) Tinker's operator-approved cleanup runs — operator-driven, applied via the operator's own clone.
  (3) PROP-026 burndown-mode bulk-closures — those write to closed-issues.json (a single file edit, not 50+ deletes).
The historical baseline (per Step 4b run reports for the last 90 days) is 0-2 deletes per cycle. A cap of 50 is 25× normal-max. False-positive rate: ~zero based on the entire historical record. If a legitimate mass-cleanup event ever fires, the gate writes a sentinel listing the proposed deletes and the operator can manually push from a clone — that's a 5-minute manual recovery, not a multi-hour disaster.
q_b_delete_gate_false_positives.edge_case: Could a section restructure (e.g. moving sections.json to a new schema) plausibly delete dozens of files? No — sections.json IS one file. A schema change is a content edit to that one file, not a delete-set. Even if a future PROP creates many per-section files and then later deletes them, that's still decider-driven and the gate doesn't apply.

q_c_depth_50_breaks_replay_scripts.the_objection: Does adding --depth 50 to workspace-sync's clone break anything that depends on full history (e.g., the closure-ledger replay scripts)?
q_c_depth_50_breaks_replay_scripts.the_response: Replay scripts read committed FILES (closure-ledger.jsonl), not git history. closure-ledger.jsonl is fully present in any clone depth — it's a file in the working tree. The PROP-045 anti-reversion historic-hash scan needs `git log -n 20` on each path; depth 50 covers it with 2.5× safety margin. No script in monitor/scripts/ or build-scripts/ requires `git log` past depth 20. Verified via grep across monitor/scripts/ and build-scripts/ — no command requires deeper history.
q_c_depth_50_breaks_replay_scripts.edge_case: If a future script ever needs full history, it can deepen the clone on demand: `git fetch --deepen=200`. That's a documented escape hatch.

q_d_pruning_breaks_tinker_audit_trail.the_objection: Does pruning monitor/integrity/ break the tinker audit-trail it depends on? What's the minimum retention window?
q_d_pruning_breaks_tinker_audit_trail.the_response:
Tinker's soft-complaints grep (per tinker.md and cost-engineering reference) scans the LAST FEW cycles of workspace-sync-runs/, narrative-cite-audit, and verify-pending-run files. The pruning policy keeps:
  - workspace-sync-runs: 30 days (~720 cycles) — well above tinker's typical 1-7 cycle scan window.
  - verify-pending-run: 14 days (~336 cycles) — covers PROP-014 amendment Q3's full acceptance window.
  - narrative-cite-audit: 7 files at full fidelity + JSONL archive of diffs going back further. Tinker's trend analysis can read the JSONL for long-tail data.
  - push-failure: 14 days (lines up with PROP-050's same audit window).
  - report-daily: 90 days at full fidelity.
All retention windows are above the longest documented tinker audit window. The archive JSONLs preserve historical data in compact form so reconstructive analysis is still possible.
q_d_pruning_breaks_tinker_audit_trail.minimum_retention: If tighter pruning is desired later, the floors below which retention should not drop: workspace-sync-runs 7 days, verify-pending-run 7 days, narrative-cite-audit 1 file, push-failure 7 days, report-daily 30 days. Recommended starting policy is conservative (longer windows) to validate that nothing breaks; tighten on evidence.

q_e_per_identity_403_model_partial_and_a.the_objection: Today's RECOVERY succeeded BECAUSE workspace-sync's identity could push fine. The disaster commit (ea785c49) also pushed fine via that identity. Doesn't that prove the per-identity-403 model is partial — some unknown shifts the auth state mid-day? If so, A is the only line of defense we control.
q_e_per_identity_403_model_partial_and_a.the_response: Conceded in full. The disaster commit pushed with the same identity that workspace-sync uses today (steve@melrosecastle.com from the workspace-sync session). PROP-050's 'decider-403 is a per-identity issue' framing is therefore INCOMPLETE — at minimum it's a per-time-window or per-network-event issue that the workspace-sync identity sometimes escapes and sometimes doesn't. We don't control which side of that asymmetry we're on. We DO control whether a commit that gets to the push stage is one we'd want to land. That makes Workstream A (the four fail-closed gates) the load-bearing defense. The patches are designed so that EACH gate is independently sufficient — A1 blocks the LLM from inventing the fallback (prompt-level), A2 blocks the run before clone (precondition), A3 blocks before any smart_copy (post-clone), A4 blocks before push (post-commit-stage). Each is a chokepoint; all four would have to fail for the disaster to recur.

q_f_what_about_the_corrupt_open_issues_j.the_objection_added_during_diagnostic: Tinker observed during this run that monitor/decisions/open-issues.json has a JSON-parse error at byte 45760 (closing array bracket before ISS-2172 entry). The previous tinker-2026-05-21T02-45 run also flagged this. Should this PROP also address that?
q_f_what_about_the_corrupt_open_issues_j.the_response: Out of scope. The corrupt open-issues.json is a separate decider-side bug. PROP-024's json-validity gate in workspace-sync would have refused the commit IF workspace-sync had been the writer — but in this case the decider's own clone committed it (FUSE-only degraded mode per ISS-2171 referenced in the partial-parse). This PROP keeps workspace-sync-side defenses; the open-issues.json corruption needs a separate PROP from the decider's own side. Noted here so it doesn't fall through cracks.

operator_unpause_checklist.title: Step-by-step gate before re-enabling workspace-sync after PROP-051 lands
operator_unpause_checklist.ordering: Run sequentially. Do not skip steps. Each step must pass before the next.

steps.label: Disk free verification
steps.fail_action: Investigate the largest consumer (likely /tmp leftover clones or monitor/integrity/). Run the Mode 2 disk audit. Do not proceed.

steps.label: A1 prompt-level prohibition present
steps.command: grep -q 'Degraded-mode prohibitions (fail-closed)' /sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo OK || echo MISSING
steps.fail_action: Patch A1 not applied. Re-apply before continuing.

steps.label: A2 disk-pressure pre-flight present
steps.command: grep -q 'Disk-pressure pre-flight (PROP-051 patch A2' /sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo OK || echo MISSING
steps.fail_action: Patch A2 not applied. Re-apply before continuing.

steps.label: A3 post-clone working-tree-size check present
steps.command: grep -q 'Working-tree population check (PROP-051 patch A3' /sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo OK || echo MISSING
steps.fail_action: Patch A3 not applied. Re-apply before continuing.

steps.label: A4 pre-push delete-sanity gate present
steps.command: grep -q 'Step 3.7: Pre-push delete-sanity gate (PROP-051 patch A4)' /sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo OK || echo MISSING
steps.fail_action: Patch A4 not applied. Re-apply before continuing.

steps.label: B1 --depth 50 on clone present
steps.command: grep -q 'git clone --depth 50 "https://x-access-token' /sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo OK || echo MISSING
steps.fail_action: Patch B1 not applied. Re-apply before continuing.

steps.label: B2 EXIT trap present
steps.command: grep -q 'trap.*rm -rf.*CLONE.*EXIT INT TERM' /sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo OK || echo MISSING
steps.fail_action: Patch B2 not applied. Re-apply before continuing.

steps.label: B3 stale-clone sweep present
steps.command: grep -q 'Stale-clone sweep (PROP-051 patch B3)' /sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo OK || echo MISSING
steps.fail_action: Patch B3 not applied. Re-apply before continuing.

steps.label: Optional but recommended: Workstream C (integrity pruning) applied
steps.pass_condition: Either 'OK' or accept 'OPTIONAL_NOT_APPLIED' if operator deferred C to a later cycle.
steps.fail_action: If 'OPTIONAL_NOT_APPLIED' and disk is currently >85% on /, do not unpause; ship C first.

steps.label: Single trial run with operator tail
steps.command: (In one terminal: tail -f /tmp/workspace-sync-trial.log) (In another: as operator-direct run, execute workspace-sync.md procedure from /tmp/edit-clone, piping output to /tmp/workspace-sync-trial.log)
steps.pass_condition: Run completes. Last log line is 'Run report written: monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/...' or 'Nothing to sync'. No 'ABORT' lines.
steps.fail_action: If 'ABORT' fires legitimately, inspect the sentinel file; resolve the cause before unpausing. If 'ABORT' fires falsely, file a follow-up PROP against the gate threshold.

steps.label: Trial commit diff-stat sanity
steps.pass_condition: stat output shows < 50 files changed total, deletes ≤ 5. Looks like a normal sync.
steps.fail_action: If deletes > 5, manually inspect each deleted path. Verify each is genuinely expected (e.g., archive flips). If not, force-reset to HEAD~1 before continuing.

steps.label: Re-enable workspace-sync scheduled task
steps.command: (via Cowork UI or operator's normal scheduled-task re-enable path)

steps.label: First post-unpause cycle audit
steps.command: After first cycle completes (within 70min of re-enable), inspect: (1) the run report at monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/run-<latest>.json — agent_notes should say 'routine' or describe specific normal observations; (2) git log --stat -1 — should show a small, sensible commit.
steps.pass_condition: Run report exists, agent_notes does not contain 'ABORT' / 'fallback' / 'improvised'. Commit stat looks normal.
steps.fail_action: If anything looks off, pause workspace-sync again immediately and triage.

tinker_diagnostic_note.diagnostic_method: FUSE + GitHub API only (per directive constraint). No clone created during this run.
